item.title: Left parties call for scrapping trade deal with US
item.description: Leaders of different left-leaningpoliticalparties on Monday urged the government to take immediate steps to scrap the trade deal signed with the United States during the tenure of the Muhammad Yunus-led interim government....Politics

item.title: Dabir-Sohani-Suraiya Foundation provides scholarships to DU students
item.description: The Dabir-Sohani-Suraiya Foundation awarded scholarships to meritorious and needy students of faculty of business studies of Dhaka University at the Professor Abdullah Farouk Multipurpose Hall on Sunday....

item.title: ‘Anindya Practan’ displays language of printmaking
item.description: A group printmaking exhibition titled Anindya Practan explored the contemporary language of printmaking....

item.title: Barishal Univ teachers begin hunger strike
item.description: The teachers of Barishal University started an agitation programme, including a fast unto death, demanding the upgradation of ranks and an end to the long-standing teachers’ shortage, aiming to continue normal educational activities in the institution....

item.title: SP, 7 other cops charged with staged gunfight, torture
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-1 on Monday framed charges against eight former police personnel, including former Jashore superintendent of police Anisur Rahman, in a crimes against humanity case over their alleged involvement in torture and a staged ‘gunfight’....Geographic Reference

item.title: Opposition leader for discussion on fuel crisis in JS
item.description: Opposition leader in the Jatiya Sangsad Shafiqur Rahman on Monday submitted an adjournment motion to the House seeking a discussion on the ongoing fuel crisis....

item.title: Economy faces risks from war on Iran, minister tells JS
item.description: Commerce minister Khandker Abdul Muktadir on Monday told the Jatiya Sangsad that the ongoing instability in the Middle East had created multiple challenges for the global economy, with potential implications for Bangladesh’s trade, import costs, shipping, inflation and......

item.title: Former DU professor Razia Sultana dies
item.description: Former Dhaka University Bangla department professor Razia Sultana passed away in Canada on Saturday....

item.title: US team on way to Pakistan, Iran yet to decide
item.description: Iran said it had yet to decide whether to attend a new round of peace negotiations with the United States on Monday, casting uncertainty on a push to stop the Middle East war from resuming....

item.title: Fuel consumers continue to suffer
item.description: Consumers’ woes in long queues at overcrowded petrol pumps in the capital Dhaka on Monday continued despite more supply while the increased prices of fuel oils created a scope for hefty profits for suppliers and dealers, said businesses....

item.title: Bangladesh again seeks safe passage thru Hormuz
item.description: Dhaka has once again urged the Iranian authorities to facilitate safe passage of vessels bound for Bangladesh, particularly those carrying fuel through the Strait of Hormuz, a major route for global energy supply, amid tensions due to the Gulf conflict....

item.title: PM warns of conspiracies
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman has urged the people of the country to remain vigilant, alleging that ‘clandestine forces’ that do not believe in Bangladesh’s existence and independence are conspiring to create unrest by misleading the public....

item.title: BRTA sends proposal to ministry to hike bus fares
item.description: The Bangladesh Road Transport Authority on Monday sent a proposal to the road transport and bridges ministry to hike the fares of the city and long-route buses....

item.title: Ruling, opposition alliances name candidates for reserved JS seats for women
item.description: The ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party-led alliance and the opposition Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami-led alliance on Monday announced the names of their nominees for the reserved seats for women in Jatiya Sangsad....

item.title: SSC exams start today
item.description: Secondary School Certificate and its equivalent examinations are beginning today across the country....

item.title: Education: hidden casualty of war
item.description: WHEN war erupts, it destroys cities, homes and lives. We see images of burning buildings, injured civilians and frightened families on the news every day. But there is another loss that often receives far less attention, the loss of children’s education. While the world focuses on battlefields andpoliticaltensions, millions of children quietly lose their classrooms, their......Politics

item.title: Cochlear implants and syndicate shadow
item.description: FOR thousands of Bangladeshi children with profound hearing loss, cochlear implants offer a pathway to language, education and social participation. Yet high costs, uneven clinical standards and largely unregulated private services continue to place this life-changing intervention beyond reach for many families......

item.title: Politics and primary responsibilities
item.description: PRE-JULY 2024 Bangladesh was a terrible mess, and the way the country is being run in the post-July 2024 era offers little hope that it will soon address its many woes and heal its many wounds. For almost all our national and local crises, we often blame one specific breed of people — politicians — who do not seem to worry too much whether we love or loathe them......City & Local Guides

item.title: Fuel policy and public burden
item.description: AT A time when Bangladesh’s economy is already under sustained stress, the latest hike in fuel and LPG prices risks pushing millions closer to economic distress. The government has raised diesel by Tk 15 per litre, octane by Tk 20, petrol by Tk 19 and kerosene by Tk 18, taking fuel prices to record highs. At the same time, LPG prices have surged well beyond regulated......

item.title: True cost of fuel price hike
item.description: EVERY civilisation has its own invisible bloodstream. In Bangladesh, that bloodstream is diesel. It irrigates the paddy field, moves the truck, powers the shallow engine, carries vegetables to the bazaar, and lights the generator when electricity fails. When the price of fuel rises, it is not merely another adjustment in an economic table. It is the moment when the cost of survival itself begins to rise......

item.title: Household service workers in need of legal framework
item.description: REGULATORY vacuum and enforcement failure in domestic household services leave both employers and workers unprotected, but not on equal terms. The High Court’s directing the government to explain its failure to implement a mandatory registration and regulatory system for such workers under the Domestic Servants Registration Ordinance 1961 brings the issue......

item.title: Mitigation measures must to help low-income people
item.description: THE increase in fuel oil prices, driven by geopolitical tension in the Middle East and disruption in the Strait of Hormuz, may be explicable in a global context. Yet, their domestic consequences have already started proving severe and far-reaching. The government on April 18 raised prices of diesel, petrol, octane and kerosene by 15–18 per cent. The immediate......

item.title: Japan issues warning after 7.7-magnitude quake
item.description: Japan issued a special advisory on Monday warning of an increased risk of earthquakes at magnitude 8.0 or stronger, after a powerful jolt rattled the country’s north and prompted a tsunami warning....

item.title: BSF detains 3 Bangladeshis along Jhenaidah border
item.description: The Border Security Force of India on Sunday detained three Bangladesh nationals in the Pargopalpur area inside Indian territory, opposite the Jadabpur Border Outpost under Maheshpur upazila in Jhenaidah....

item.title: $71b needed over next decade to rebuild Gaza: UN, EU
item.description: More than $71 billion will be needed over the next decade for recovery and reconstruction in war-ravaged Gaza, according to an EU-UN assessment published Monday....

item.title: Block 6 credit cards of former mayor Taposh, wife: court
item.description: The Dhaka Metropolitan Senior Special Judge Court on Monday ordered the Anti-Corruption Commission to block the debit transactions on six credit cards held at Madhumati Bank PLC in the names of former Dhaka South City Corporation mayor Sheikh Fazle Noor Taposh and his wife, Afrin Taposh, over allegations of corruption....City & Local Guides

item.title: Tarique Siddique’s staffers remanded for July uprising killing
item.description: A Dhaka court on Monday placed two personal staffers of ousted prime minister Sheikh Hasina’s former Defence and Security adviser, major general (Retd.) Tarique Ahmed Siddique, on a four-day remand each in a case lodged over the killing of trader Abdul Wadud during the July Mass Uprising....

item.title: Female farmer dies while returning from filling station
item.description: A female farmer, who had been standing in the queue for diesel for a long time at a fuel station, died on her way home on Monday....

item.title: Probe body to be formed over ‘inflated prices’ of purchases for JS
item.description: Officials of the Jatiya Sangsad secretariat on Monday said that a high-level investigation committee would be formed in a day or two over a report published in a Bangla daily regarding ‘rampant irregularities in procurement for parliament’....

item.title: HC defers hearing on writ petition against secretariat abolition law
item.description: The High Court on Monday deferred the hearing of a writ petition challenging the Supreme Court Secretariat (Abolition) Act, 2026, pending a decision in a related matter before the Appellate Division....

item.title: Govt has no intention to control media: state minister
item.description: State minister for information and broadcasting Yasser Khan Chowdhury on Monday said that the government had no intention of controlling the media....

item.title: Govt aims to end exam fear to promote joyful education: Mahdi Amin
item.description: Prime minister’s adviser to the Ministry of Education Mahdi Amin on Monday said that the government wanted to eliminate exam fear from the minds of students as well as guardians and conduct examinations in a joyful and positive atmosphere....

item.title: Army chief leaves for Malaysia on official visit
item.description: Army chief General Waker-Uz-Zaman left Dhaka for Malaysia on Sunday on a four-day official visit....Geographic Reference

item.title: BFSA chair urges people to stop using burnt cooking oil
item.description: Bangladesh Food Safety Authority chairman Md Anowarul Islam Sarkar at a seminar in Dhaka on Monda urged people to stop using burnt cooking oil....

item.title: Probe body formed to investigate rice distribution irregularities
item.description: A one-member probe body has been formed to investigate allegations of irregularities in the distribution of rice among fishermen at Daulatkhan in Bhola....

item.title: Coast Guard seizes illegal nets worth Tk 312cr in Ctg
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard in a joint drive with the Bangladesh Navy and the Department of Fisheries seized illegal fishing nets worth approximately Tk 312 crore at Karnaphuli in Chattogram on Monday....

item.title: 17.71 lakh tonnes of food grains in state warehouses, govt tells JS
item.description: State minister for food Md Abdul Bari on Monday told the Jatiya Sangsad that as of April 13, the country had 17.71 lakh tonnes of food grains in government warehouses....

item.title: 2 held over rape, filming at Savar
item.description: The police have detained two young men at Savar, on the outskirts of Dhaka, over allegations of rape of a young woman and filming of the assault, officials said on Sunday, as search continues for other suspects....

item.title: CID files money laundering case against seven of same family
item.description: The Criminal Investigation Department has filed a case against seven of a family under the Money Laundering Prevention Act, 2012 with the Tongi East police....

item.title: Workshop held for journalists on child, adolescent, women development
item.description: A three-day workshop titled ‘Essential information for life through field-level practice’ under the ‘Awareness promotion activities for child, adolescent and women development’ project concluded in Manikganj....

item.title: BMU holds seminar on World Hormone Day 2026
item.description: The Bangladesh Medical University organised a university central seminar on World Hormone Day 2026 at the BMU in Dhaka on Monday....

item.title: SMUCT holds seminar on reshaping banking sector
item.description: The Shanto-Mariam University of Creative Technology held a seminar titled Reshaping the Banking Sector in Bangladesh: The Role of FinTech and AI at the SMUCT in Dhaka on Monday, focusing on the modernisation and technology-driven future of Bangladesh’s banking industry....

item.title: Politician Shamsuddoha dies
item.description: Left leader and former member of parliament Mohammad Shamsuddoha died on Monday noon in a city hospital....
